etymology.description: phonetic 小(xiǎo, "small") + semantic 肉(ròu, "meat") -> resemble
etymology.interpretation: 肖(xiào, "resemble") combines the phonetic element 小(xiǎo, "small") to suggest its pronunciation with the semantic element 肉(ròu, "meat") indicating a connection to physical appearance. The core meaning 'resemble' arises from the idea of something appearing similar to another, as meat can take on various appearances, enhanced by the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 这幅画很好地描绘了他的肖像(這幅畫很好地描繪了他的肖像)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fú huà hěn hǎo de miáohuì le tā de xiàoxiàng.
examples.english: This painting excellently depicts his likeness.

examples.chinese: 他长得很像他的父亲(他長得很像他的父親)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhǎng de hěn xiàng tā de fùqīn.

examples.chinese: 这个模型逼真地模仿了原物(這個模型逼真地模仿了原物)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège móxíng bīzhēn de mófǎng le yuánwù.
examples.english: This model realistically imitates the original.
